title: Win the Center Pawn
setup: Black has played **...d5**, offering a pawn trade in the center. White accepts.
hint: When you can take a central pawn for free, almost always do it.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is the most fundamental **central capture** in chess opening play. Black's d5 pawn is attacked by White's e4 pawn but defended by... nothing in particular.

White plays {{exd5}}, winning the d-pawn outright.

## Why It's Good

- White now has a pawn on **d5**, dominating central space.
- Black's e5 pawn is now isolated and weak.
- Material: White is up one pawn.

If Black plays {{Nxd5}} or {{Qxd5}} to recapture, the recapturing piece is exposed to attack (e.g., by {{Nc3}} hitting the queen).

## Pattern to Memorize

> A central pawn (especially **d-pawn or e-pawn**) is worth more than a wing pawn. When you can grab one and keep it, do.

White has converted a tactical capture into a strategic plus — extra material *plus* central control.
